title: Classify Real Implementations
summary: Classifies the current repository surfaces that contain source-backed, executable TypeScript implementations rather than simplified launcher glue, src/playable contract artifacts, pending oracle fixtures, or plan manifest gates. This is source-only metadata: it proves local implementation surface area and test coverage, not final side-by-side vanilla parity.
evidence_method: inventory lane source-only audit using src/, test/, tools/reference/, plan_engine/, plan_fps/, reference/manifests/, and package.json; counted committed TypeScript and bun:test files with git ls-files from local repository paths and sampled representative modules/tests without embedding proprietary WAD or executable bytes
implications: The repository already contains substantial real implementation modules across bootstrap, core, WAD/assets, map/world, player/AI/specials, renderer/UI, audio, save/config/demo, input, and oracle support surfaces.

This classification does not prove the simplified launcher reaches those implementations through the final vanilla `bun run doom.ts` path; launch, oracle, and acceptance lanes must still wire and compare behavior.
The src/playable/ contract tree, src/main.ts launcher path, src/launcher/ host path, plan_fps manifests, and pending fixture gates need separate classification before 01-024 can gate the inventory.

criteria_sorted: has executable TypeScript source under an implementation root
has local bun:test coverage or vanilla_parity audit coverage
models a named DOOM or support-system behavior rather than only declaring a plan gate
uses local repository, prior plan, and manifest evidence without proprietary byte embedding
classification_definition.scope_note: A real classification here means source-backed implementation evidence exists. It is not a claim that the final vanilla runtime path or reference oracle comparison already passes.

excluded_surfaces.reason: oracle fixture and parity fixture manifests can encode pending expectations without executable implementation behavior
prior plan gate manifests and acceptance placeholders document intent and gaps rather than runtime implementation
01-001 and 01-002 already identify the current launcher as simplified and not the final vanilla D_DoomMain path
01-004 records src/playable/ as a contract surface with no non-playable src/ importers and no direct runtime path

real_implementation_groups.evidence: src/audio/ modules implement SFX channel allocation, DMX sample expansion/mixing, MUS parsing/scheduling, music state, OPL register/synthesis models, sound origins, and spatialization with matching test/audio coverage.
real_implementation_groups.notes: Audio behavior remains downstream-gated by 11-* oracle comparison steps, but these modules are executable implementations rather than manifests.

real_implementation_groups.evidence: src/bootstrap/ contains executable command-line, game-mode, init-order, quit, title-loop, tic, and launch-policy implementations, including vanilla_parity launch audit modules with focused tests.
real_implementation_groups.notes: 01-003 records that this surface is not yet wired into src/mainLoop.ts, so it is real module evidence but not final launch proof.

real_implementation_groups.evidence: src/core/, src/host/, and src/mainLoop.ts provide fixed-point math, angle wrapping, deterministic RNG, trig lookup, binary reading, tic accumulation, Win32 timing/message pump helpers, and frame-loop orchestration with core/host/vanilla_parity tests.
real_implementation_groups.notes: This surface is the determinism boundary used by downstream gameplay systems and remains source-backed even where final orchestrator wiring still needs launch-lane proof.

real_implementation_groups.evidence: src/input/, src/oracles/, and tools/reference/ contain executable keyboard/mouse/ticcmd, capture-format, sandbox, hash, music-event, input-script, and Win32 reference probe implementations with input/oracle tests.
real_implementation_groups.notes: Oracle support is real support code, but captured oracle data and pending fixture manifests are separately excluded from this real-implementation classification.

real_implementation_groups.evidence: src/map/ and src/world/ implement map lump structures, setup, blockmap/BSP traversal, intercepts, mobj state, collision, line use, teleport, movement, and thinker behavior with map/world tests.
real_implementation_groups.notes: This classification is backed by local source and tests plus reference manifest anchors for map lump order and vanilla limits.

real_implementation_groups.evidence: src/ai/, src/player/, and src/specials/ implement monster targeting/attacks/chase behavior, player spawn/weapons/pickups, and sector/line/door/floor/platform/switch specials with ai/player/specials tests.
real_implementation_groups.notes: These gameplay modules are source-backed implementation surfaces, with final live-game parity still dependent on downstream core/map/render/audio integration gates.

real_implementation_groups.evidence: src/render/ and src/ui/ implement column/span drawing, visplanes, wall/sprite/masked rendering, fuzz, sky, menus, status bar, automap, HUD, finale, intermission, and UI asset handling with render/ui tests.
real_implementation_groups.notes: Rendering and UI surfaces contain executable implementations, while framebuffer and menu-oracle parity remains gated by downstream render/UI comparison steps.

real_implementation_groups.evidence: src/config/, src/demo/, and src/save/ implement default/host config parsing, demo parse/playback/recording, vanilla save headers, core serialization, loadgame, special serialization, and limits with config/demo/save tests.
real_implementation_groups.notes: The modules provide real serialization/parsing behavior, while byte-for-byte save and demo oracle comparisons remain downstream acceptance work.

real_implementation_groups.evidence: src/assets/ and src/wad/ implement WAD headers/directories/lump lookup, marker ranges, PLAYPAL/COLORMAP, PNAMES, TEXTURE1/TEXTURE2, patches, flats, sprites, MUS/SFX/demo/map lump parsing, IWAD capability checks, and asset boundary enforcement with wad/reference/vanilla_parity tests.
real_implementation_groups.notes: This group is classified real because it parses and validates concrete data structures without embedding proprietary bytes; runtime asset-cache wiring remains downstream WAD-lane work.
